top_signals.title: Cash Crunch Leaves Braskem’s Mexico Unit Missing Out on Oil Boom
top_signals.summary: Braskem SA’s beleaguered petrochemical Mexico unit is missing out on the energy prices boom, hobbled by a cash crunch even as investors push its bonds higher.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: UK Energy Bills Set to Surge as Middle East Crisis Ripples Through Gas Markets
top_signals.summary: Brits are set to face a sharp hike in energy costs this summer, with household bills forecast to rise by more than £200 a year, as the conflict in the Middle East ripples into the UK market. Energy analyst Cornwall Insight predicts that the annual bill for a typical dual-fuel household will increase to £1,850, according to its final forecast for the July to September price cap. This represents a 13 per cent increase from the current cap of £1,641. The rise has been credited to the volatile wholesale market, which has been damaged…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.
